title: OBD-II Standard PID Reference (SAE J1979 Mode $01)
description: Complete reference for SAE J1979 Mode $01 PIDs available on all OBD-II vehicles. Used by ProcedureEngineer to select correct live data parameters for any diagnostic workflow.
physical_solution: Query Mode $01 supported PIDs first (PID $00, $20, $40, $60) then read desired parameters. All formulas produce engineering units from raw hex response byte A.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Full — all Mode $01 PIDs universally accessible on every OBD-II vehicle 1996+ via ELM327 AT commands. No gateway restrictions apply to Mode $01.

procedure_steps.description: PID $00: Supported PIDs [01-20]. Query first to determine which PIDs supported. Formula: bit mask decode. ELM327: 01 00.
PID $04: Calculated Engine Load. Formula: A/2.55 = % (0-100%). Normal: 15-25% idle, 70-90% WOT. Units: percent.
PID $05: Engine Coolant Temperature. Formula: A-40 = °C. Normal: 80-100°C. Below 60°C = not at operating temp. Above 110°C = overheating concern. Units: °C.
PID $06: Short Term Fuel Trim Bank 1 (STFT B1). Formula: (A-128)/1.28 = %. Normal: ±5%. Concern: >+10% (lean) or <-10% (rich). Units: percent.
PID $07: Long Term Fuel Trim Bank 1 (LTFT B1). Formula: (A-128)/1.28 = %. Normal: ±5%. Action required: >+15% or <-15%. Units: percent.
PID $08: Short Term Fuel Trim Bank 2 (STFT B2). Formula: (A-128)/1.28 = %. V6/V8 only. Same ranges as B1. Units: percent.
PID $09: Long Term Fuel Trim Bank 2 (LTFT B2). Formula: (A-128)/1.28 = %. V6/V8 only. Same ranges as B1. Units: percent.
PID $0A: Fuel Pressure (gauge). Formula: A*3 = kPa. Normal port injection: 270-410 kPa (39-60 psi). Units: kPa.

PID $0B: Intake Manifold Absolute Pressure (MAP). Formula: A = kPa. Normal at idle: 20-50 kPa. At WOT naturally aspirated: near ambient (~100 kPa). Turbocharged at boost: can exceed 200 kPa. Units: kPa.
PID $0C: Engine RPM. Formula: (256*A+B)/4 = RPM. Normal idle: 600-900 RPM. Units: RPM. Two bytes (A and B).
PID $0D: Vehicle Speed. Formula: A = km/h. Divide by 1.609 for mph. Units: km/h.
PID $0E: Timing Advance. Formula: A/2 - 64 = degrees BTDC. Normal idle: 8-20° BTDC. Knock retard: drops negative. Units: degrees.
PID $0F: Intake Air Temperature (IAT). Formula: A-40 = °C. Should be close to ambient when cold. High IAT at operating temp = MAF housing heat soak or charge air cooler issue. Units: °C.
PID $10: Mass Air Flow Rate (MAF). Formula: (256*A+B)/100 = g/s. Normal idle 2.5-4.5 g/s. At WOT scales with engine size. Low for engine size = dirty MAF or air restriction. Units: g/s. Two bytes.
PID $11: Throttle Position (absolute). Formula: A/2.55 = %. Idle: 8-15%. WOT: 90-100%. Units: percent.
PID $1F: Run Time Since Engine Start. Formula: 256*A+B = seconds. Units: seconds. Two bytes.
PID $21: Distance Traveled with MIL On. Formula: 256*A+B = km. Units: km. Two bytes.
PID $2C: Commanded EGR. Formula: A/2.55 = %. Normal: 0% idle, increases at cruise. Units: percent.
PID $2D: EGR Error. Formula: (A-128)/1.28 = %. Normal: ±5%. Large deviation = stuck EGR valve. Units: percent.
PID $2F: Fuel Tank Level Input. Formula: A/2.55 = %. Units: percent.
PID $33: Absolute Barometric Pressure. Formula: A = kPa. Normal sea level: 101 kPa. High altitude: lower. Units: kPa.
PID $42: Control Module Voltage. Formula: (256*A+B)/1000 = V. Normal running: 13.5-14.7V. Key ON engine OFF: 12.0-12.8V. Units: volts. Two bytes.
PID $45: Relative Throttle Position. Formula: A/2.55 = %. Relative to learned closed position. More useful than absolute for throttle diagnosis. Units: percent.
PID $46: Ambient Air Temperature. Formula: A-40 = °C. Units: °C.
PID $5C: Engine Oil Temperature. Formula: A-40 = °C. Normal operating: 90-120°C. High: >130°C concern. Units: °C.
PID $5E: Engine Fuel Rate. Formula: (256*A+B)/20 = L/h. Useful for fuel consumption monitoring. Units: L/h. Two bytes.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: All standard PIDs supported. Toyota enhanced PIDs available via $22 at 0x7E0 for A/F sensor ratio, VVT angles, injector pulse width, knock count.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: All standard PIDs supported. Honda enhanced via $22 at 0x7E8 for VTEC status, cam timing, individual cylinder data.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: All standard PIDs supported. Nissan enhanced via CONSULT addressing for CVT data, ATTESA, per-cylinder trims.
oem_notes.Ford: All standard PIDs supported. Ford enhanced via $22 for EcoBoost boost pressure, turbo speed, injector timing.
oem_notes.GM: All standard PIDs supported. GM enhanced via $22 for AFM/DOD status, individual cylinder fuel trims, transmission data.
oem_notes.BMW: All standard PIDs supported. BMW enhanced (non-OBD) via $22 to DME 0x12 for Valvetronic lift, VANOS angles, adaptation maps.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: All standard PIDs supported. VAG enhanced via measuring blocks (VCDS) or $22 for DSG data, DPF soot load, injector corrections.
oem_notes.Stellantis: All standard PIDs supported. Chrysler enhanced via $22 for individual cylinder contribution, EGR position, turbo vane.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: All standard PIDs supported. Hyundai enhanced via $22 for CVVT position, dual injection status, knock per cylinder.
